feature_name: Dual operation modes: Root Complex and End Point
description: The controller operates as either a Root Complex or an End Point with a software selectable mode. Bootstrap pins select the default; software can overwrite via configuration. In EP mode it supports legacy or native endpoint behavior. In RC mode it manages downstream configuration and memory mapping. Operation is compliant to PCIe Base Specification 2.0.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; Section 1.4.1 PCIe Core Module; PCIE_CAP[DPORT_TYPE]
dependencies: Proper mode strap or DEVSTAT write; LTSSM enable; stable reference clock
Remarks: Select mode early in init; verify LTSSM state reaches L0 before issuing transactions.

feature_name: Link width and speed: x1/x2; 2.5/5.0 GT/s; lane reversal and polarity inversion
description: A single bidirectional link supports x1 or x2 lanes with dynamic width conversion. Gen1 2.5 GT/s and Gen2 5.0 GT/s are supported with automatic lane reversal and RX polarity inversion. Speed changes are directed through PL_GEN2 and Link Control 2. The PHY provides configurable de-emphasis and swing for Gen2. Hardware sets negotiated width and speed in status registers.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; LINK_CAP[MAX_LINK_WIDTH; MAX_LINK_SPEED]; LINK_STAT_CTRL[NEGOTIATED_LINK_WD; LINK_SPEED]; PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD; DEEMPH; CFG_TX_SWING]
dependencies: SerDes configured and PLL locked; common clock settings as needed
Remarks: Program Gen1 first; then use DIR_SPD to retrain to Gen2; validate negotiated width and error margins.

feature_name: Endian conversion for inbound and outbound transactions
description: The subsystem supports big and little endian modes even though the core is little endian on pins. Programmable byte swap modes apply to inbound and outbound TLPs. ENDIAN register selects 1/2/4/8 byte swizzle to meet system memory layout. Byte enables are swapped consistently with data. Use little endian mode to avoid conversion overhead when possible.
evidence: Section 2.10.2; Table 2-8; Table 2-9; ENDIAN[ENDIAN_MODE]
dependencies: System endianness selection at boot; ENDIAN register set
Remarks: Confirm software buffers and DMA descriptors match selected swizzle; test both read and write paths.

feature_name: Outbound address translation: 32 regions of 1–8 MB
description: Outbound address translation maps internal bus addresses to PCIe memory addresses. Thirty two equal sized regions are configured with OB_SIZE and per-region OB_OFFSET registers. Translations support 32-bit and 64-bit targets using OB_OFFSETn_HI. Transactions that cross a region boundary have unspecified behavior and must be bounded. Software initializes regions before enabling transactions.
evidence: Section 2.7.1; OB_SIZE; OB_OFFSET_INDEXn; OB_OFFSETn_HI; Example 2-1
dependencies: Set CMD_STATUS[OB_XLT_EN]; program all active regions; ensure SerDes operational
Remarks: Choose region size to cover all windows without boundary crossings; document mapping for DMA engines.

feature_name: Inbound address translation: 4 regions and BAR matching
description: Inbound translation remaps accepted PCIe addresses into internal memory ranges. Four regions each associate with a BAR and define start and offset; BAR0 always maps to application registers. BAR1 value can be used directly as a start address for one window when programmed to zero in START. Regions enable mapping of multiple non-contiguous ranges by ordered starts.
evidence: Section 2.7.2; IB_BARn; IB_STARTn_LO/HI; IB_OFFSETn; BAR0 exception; Example 2-2; Table 2-6
dependencies: Set CMD_STATUS[IB_XLT_EN]; configure BARs; coordinate with RC enumeration
Remarks: Do not remap BAR0; validate 32-bit vs 64-bit addressing pairs; ensure ordered START addresses for range stitching.

feature_name: BAR Mask overlay and programmable BAR sizing
description: BAR Mask registers overlay the BARs to define requested window sizes and types prior to enumeration. DBI_CS2 in CMD_STATUS enables temporary writes to BAR masks. Mask registers are write-only; verification is by writing patterns to BARs and observing masked bits. BAR0 mask is limited to bits [31:20] as BAR0 is dedicated to application registers.
evidence: Section 2.7.3.1; CMD_STATUS[DBI_CS2]; BARx_MASK entries
dependencies: DBI_CS2 gating; correct Type 0 or Type 1 header context
Remarks: Enable DBI_CS2 only during pre-enumeration setup; always read-back to confirm effect; never modify via serial side.

feature_name: DMA through VBUSM master/slave with EDMA examples
description: The PCIe block exposes a 128-bit VBUSM slave for outbound DMA and a 128-bit master for inbound requests. No internal DMA is provided; external EDMA or CPU initiates bursts over the slave port. Bandwidth planning targets about 85% of 2.0 or 4.0 Gbps per lane after 8b/10b coding. Reference examples show EDMA setup for memory read and write with 128 byte payloads. Address translation regions map device addresses for DMA windows.
evidence: Section 1.4.3; Section 2.9; Examples 2-4 and 2-5
dependencies: Configured outbound/inbound translation; LTSSM in L0; EDMA driver present
Remarks: Align transfers to 128 bytes; throttle to payload sizes; validate completion handling under backpressure.

feature_name: Interrupt generation in EP: Legacy and MSI
description: As an End Point the controller can generate either legacy INTx messages or MSI writes to the RC. Legacy asserts via EP_IRQ_SET and deasserts via EP_IRQ_CLR; only one pending interrupt is allowed. MSI uses RC-provisioned address and data; writing a vector value to MSI_IRQ triggers the corresponding MSI event. MSI and legacy are mutually exclusive per PCIe rules.
evidence: Section 2.14.2; EP_IRQ_SET/CLR/STATUS; MSI_CAP; MSI_LOW32; MSI_UP32; MSI_DATA; MSI_IRQ
dependencies: RC must program MSI capability and target; BAR0 must be reachable; link up
Remarks: Prefer MSI for scalability; clear status and write EOI after service; avoid re-trigger before deassert or status clear.

feature_name: Interrupt reception in RC: Legacy and MSI with event mapping
description: In RC mode the controller receives INTx and MSI from multiple EPs and maps them to 14 interrupt events. Status, enable, and EOI registers control delivery and completion. MSI events are grouped in banks of four per CPU interrupt event. Software must account for relaxed ordering and ensure data writes complete before handling MSI.
evidence: Section 2.14.1; 2.14.5; Tables 2-10; IRQ_EOI; MSIn_IRQ_STATUS/ENABLE; LEGACY_X*
dependencies: RC configuration space initialized; IRQ routing to CPU set; optional RELAXED control
Remarks: Disable relaxed ordering if ordering hazards exist; probe EP status to disambiguate shared vectors.

feature_name: Device and link power management: D-states and ASPM L0s/L1
description: The device supports PCI power states D0 to D3hot and D3cold; link ASPM states L0s and L1 are supported. Hardware and software control transitions; L0s and L1 entry can be enabled and latencies are programmable. Relationship between device and link states is defined to ensure safe combinations. L2 is not supported; L3 is power off by default for system control.
evidence: Sections 2.15; Figures 2-6 to 2-8; Table 2-11; LINK_CAP[AS_LINK_PM; L0s/L1]; ACK_FREQ[L1/L0s entry]
dependencies: RC policy; ASPM enable in capability; clock architecture choice
Remarks: Coordinate device D-states with link ASPM; program entry latencies conservatively; verify wake paths in system.

feature_name: Baseline and Advanced Error Reporting with logging
description: Baseline PCI-compatible status and PCIe Device Status report correctable; non-fatal; and fatal errors. Advanced Error Reporting provides fine grained status; masks; severity control; and header logging. Root ports can route SERR on selected errors and issue PME interrupts. First Error Pointer identifies the earliest fault to aid triage.
evidence: Sections 2.16.1–2.16.2.3; PCIE_UNCERR; PCIE_UNCERR_MASK; PCIE_UNCERR_SVRTY; PCIE_CERR; PCIE_CERR_MASK; PCIE_ACCR; ROOT_ERR_CMD; ROOT_ERR_ST; HDR_LOGx
dependencies: Enable reporting bits in DEV_STAT_CTRL and ROOT_CTRL_CAP; configure masks and severity
Remarks: Start with conservative masks; log headers on uncorrectable conditions; integrate to system RAS flow.

feature_name: ECRC generation and checking control
description: End to End CRC can be generated and checked under software control. PCIE_ACCR exposes capability and enables for generation and checking. ECRC errors map into Advanced Error Reporting status and can be masked or classified in severity. Feature should be disabled if the link partner does not support ECRC to avoid false errors.
evidence: Section 2.16.2.3.1; PCIE_ACCR[ECRC_GEN_EN; ECRC_CHK_EN]
dependencies: Both ends must agree on ECRC usage; correct capability advertisement
Remarks: Check partner capability during enumeration; disable if unsupported; test with injected errors using DIAG_CTRL.

feature_name: PIPE and PHY loopback for validation
description: The controller supports PIPE loopback in both RC and EP roles with master or slave mode. PHY loopback is available in RC and can loop traffic without a link partner by enabling SerDes loopback and forcing LTSSM state. Configuration requires setting SerDes CFG registers and PL_FORCE_LINK to POLL_ACTIVE for PHY mode. Loopback is for test; not for normal TLP traffic on PIPE mode.
evidence: Section 2.12; SERDES_CFG0/1[TX_LOOPBACK; RX_LOOPBACK]; PL_LINK_CTRL[LPBK_EN]; PL_FORCE_LINK[LINK_STATE; FORCE_LINK]
dependencies: Stable PLL; correct LTSSM sequencing; disable timers as needed
Remarks: Set loopback before any traffic to avoid sequence mismatches; ensure BAR windows avoid internal register hits.

feature_name: Reset and power domain control with hot reset support
description: Hardware supports power-on reset; system reset; and software hot reset via RSTCMD. Hot reset propagates downstream only and requires waiting 100 ms minimum before subsequent accesses. Power domain cycling resets the module and requires link reinitialization; all traffic must be quiesced before gating. Sticky bits survive soft reset as documented.
evidence: Section 2.13; RSTCMD[INIT_RST; FLUSH_N]; PMRST_*; CMD_STATUS; PSC notes
dependencies: PSC or equivalent power controller; software timers; interrupt handlers
Remarks: Always check FLUSH_N and activity status before reset; honor required settle times; re-run full init sequence.

feature_name: Clocking and SerDes PLL configuration and lock detection
description: A differential reference clock feeds the SerDes PLL to generate PHY and bit clocks. The PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL configures bandwidth; multiply; and enables; and PCIE_SERDES_STS[LOCK] indicates readiness. Common clock architecture affects training sequences and must be declared in LINK_STAT_CTRL[COMMON_CLK_CFG] when not used. Operations cannot proceed until the PLL is locked and stable.
evidence: Section 2.2–2.4; PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL; PCIE_SERDES_STS[LOCK]; LINK_STAT_CTRL[COMMON_CLK_CFG]
dependencies: Stable refclk; correct LB; MPY; and ENPLL; kicker unlock if required
Remarks: Gate configuration writes on LOCK; prefer common clock with SSC backplanes; document jitter budgets and LB settings.

feature_name: Directed Gen2 speed change and Gen2 controls
description: Software can direct a speed change from Gen1 to Gen2 after link up using PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD]. De-emphasis; swing; and number of FTS are programmable to meet channel characteristics. Link Control 2 selects de-emphasis and compliance patterns for validation. Hardware records the active de-emphasis state for diagnostics.
evidence: PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD; DEEMPH; CFG_TX_SWING; NUM_FTS]; LINK_CTRL2[SEL_DEEMPH; ENTR_COMPL; TGT_SPEED]
dependencies: Link must be stable at Gen1; partner must support Gen2; margins sufficient
Remarks: Retrain during maintenance windows; tune NUM_FTS and de-emphasis for eye opening; confirm negotiated speed.

feature_name: Configuration access windows and IO TLP base
description: Address Space 0 provides 4 KB regions for application registers; local config; remote config; and remote IO. CFG_SETUP selects bus; device; function; and type for outbound config cycles; IOBASE holds the base for outbound IO requests. Software must avoid remote windows when link is down to prevent hangs. All accesses must be 32-bit and non-burst in these regions.
evidence: Section 2.8.1; CFG_SETUP; IOBASE; Table 2-7; caution note
dependencies: LTSSM in L0; correct privileges; RC enumeration state
Remarks: Add link-status guards around remote CFG/IO; serialize 32-bit CSRs; keep remote IO windows minimal in RC.

feature_name: Transaction requirements: alignment; burst type; and byte strobe
description: Internal bus transactions must be 128 bytes or smaller; misaligned writes reduce max size to 120 bytes. Fixed and wrap bursts are not supported; only incremental bursts are allowed. Read interleaving is avoided by keeping read sizes within configured max transaction size. Byte enables must be contiguous ones with no holes within a beat. Violations may cause unspecified behavior or bus lock up.
dependencies: DMA engine settings; CPU store rules; payload config
Remarks: Program DMA to 128B aligned bursts; mask writes to contiguous bytes; test corner cases under stress.

feature_name: Debug visibility via DEBUG registers and LTSSM state map
description: DEBUG0 and DEBUG1 provide PIPE signal visibility; link idle; training; and LTSSM state fields. The appendix maps encoded LTSSM states to human readable names. Status bits show TS1; TS2; lane reversal; compliance; and electrical idle indications. These registers are read-only and update continuously.
evidence: DEBUG0; DEBUG1; Appendix A Table A-1
dependencies: None
Remarks: Poll during bring-up to identify stuck states; add firmware tracing of LTSSM code and partner capabilities.

feature_name: Max payload and request sizes; performance guidance
description: Module supports maximum outbound payload of 128 bytes and inbound of 256 bytes with remote read request up to 256 bytes. Practical throughput planning is ~85 percent of 2.0 or 4.0 Gbps per lane after encoding overhead. Device and link parameters like RCB and Max Read Request affect completion pacing. Tune sizes to the system DMA and cacheline boundaries.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; DEV_STAT_CTRL[MAX_REQ_SZ; MAX_PAYLD]; LINK_STAT_CTRL[RCB]
dependencies: Coordinated settings across EP and RC; DMA payload config
Remarks: Keep RCB consistent across link; increase MRRS only if buffers allow; validate fairness under mixed traffic.

feature_name: Security capabilities
description: The specification does not describe security features such as IOMMU, address isolation, TLP encryption or access control. Reported mechanisms (parity, AER) are reliability features, not security. Therefore no explicit security controls are documented in this controller specification.
evidence: Not specified; see Section 2.16 (error handling) for RAS (not security)
dependencies: UNKNOWN
Remarks: Use system-level protections (IOMMU, firewalls, ACS/ARI in switches) as needed; threat model and mitigations are platform-dependent.
